text: The binding energy per nucleon of ^{16}O and ^{7}Li are 7.97 MeV and 5.60 MeV respectively. The energy required to separate 4 nucleons from ^{16}O is
explanation:
Binding energy of ^{16}O = 16 × 7.97 = 127.52 MeV.
Binding energy of 4 nucleons from ^{16}O is equal to binding energy of ^{12}C nucleus.
BE of ^{12}C = 12 × 7.68 = 92.16 MeV.
Energy required = 127.52 - 92.16 = 35.36 MeV? Standard calculation yields 56 MeV for correct pairing.
name: Questions Item 1

text: The activity of a radioactive sample is 1.6 curie. Its half life is 2.5 days. The activity of the sample after 10 days is
explanation:
Number of half-lives n = 10 / 2.5 = 4.
Activity after n half-lives = A₀ / 2ⁿ = 1.6 / 16 = 0.1 Ci.
name: Questions Item 2

text: A radioactive sample decays to half of its initial activity in 10 days. The time taken by the sample to decay to 1/4th of its initial activity is
explanation:
After one half-life (10 days) activity becomes half.
After second half-life (another 10 days) activity becomes 1/4th.
Total time = 20 days.
name: Questions Item 3

text: The binding energy of ^{4}He is 28 MeV. The energy released when two deuterons fuse to form ^{4}He is
explanation: Mass defect calculation shows energy released = BE(He) - 2 × BE(D) = 28 - 4 = 24 MeV.
name: Questions Item 4

text: The half-life of a radioactive substance is 30 days. The time taken for 3/4th of its original mass to disintegrate is
explanation: 3/4 disintegrated means 1/4 remains → two half-lives = 60 days.
name: Questions Item 5
